title: Solid lotion bar
subtitle: A lavender-scented bar of beeswax, shea butter, and cocoa butter that melts on contact with skin
excerpt: A solid lotion bar is a waterless skin conditioner: equal weights of beeswax, shea butter, and cocoa butter melted together, scented with lavender, and poured into tins to set solid. It softens and melts on contact with warm skin, leaving a conditioning film without the greasiness of a heavy balm. Yield is four bars of around 60 g each.
sourceNotes: Solid lotion bar formula: 1:1:1 ratio of beeswax, shea butter, cocoa butter is the standard starting formula. Beeswax provides the solid structure; shea and cocoa butter give the emollient, conditioning feel. Fragrance oil at 2.9% of total weight; essential oil at same rate. No preservative required: water-free (anhydrous) formulation. Lavender EO (Lavandula angustifolia) in leave-on products: IFRA standard allows up to 17% in a leave-on body product; 2.9% is well within limits.

recipe.batchNotes: Double the batch to fill a second set of tins. Proportions hold at double.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Set within 2 hours at room temperature; ready to use as soon as solid.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months in a cool, dark place. Cocoa butter and shea have a natural antioxidant resistance, but the bars will develop a slight whitening (fat bloom) if stored in a warm place. Discard if they develop a rancid or off smell.

glossaryTerms.definition: A formulation containing no water. Anhydrous preparations do not require a preservative because bacteria and mould need water to grow. Solid lotion bars, balms, and salves are all anhydrous.

content.text: A solid lotion bar is an

skin conditioner: because it contains no water, it does not require a preservative and has a long shelf life. The formula uses equal weights of beeswax, shea butter, and cocoa butter. Beeswax gives the bar structure and a melting point above room temperature; shea and cocoa butter provide the emollient feel and conditioning properties. On contact with warm skin, the bar softens and melts, delivering a thin film of nourishing butter. It absorbs without a heavy residue. Lavender essential oil at 2.9% of the total weight gives a clean, calm scent.

items.prepNote: Cosmetic-grade beeswax; provides the solid structure
Unrefined gives a nuttier scent; refined is more neutral
Food-grade cosmetic cocoa butter; adds a faint chocolate note
2.9% of total weight; add at 55°C, once off the heat

content.text: Place the beeswax, shea butter, and cocoa butter in a heatproof bowl over a pan of barely simmering water. Stir occasionally until fully melted. The beeswax sets the melting point for the mixture; it takes the longest to melt. Heat until the mixture is fully liquid and smooth, around 70 to 75°C. Remove from the heat.
Add the essential oil and pour

Allow to cool to around 55°C, then add the lavender essential oil and stir to combine. Pour into the salve tins, filling to just below the rim. Work quickly; the mixture sets fast once poured into cold tins.
Leave the tins undisturbed at room temperature until fully set, at least 2 hours. Do not move or jostle the tins while the mixture is setting. Once solid, lid the tins and label.

Warm the bar between your palms for a few seconds, then smooth over dry skin: elbows, knees, hands, heels, or any dry patch. A small amount goes a long way. For very dry areas, use after a warm shower when the skin is still warm. The bar leaves a light film that absorbs within a few minutes.

Store in a cool, dark place with the lid on. The bar is stable for 12 months. If stored in a warm room the bar may soften or develop fat bloom (a whitish patchy surface); this is cosmetic and does not affect the product. Discard if the bar develops a rancid or off smell.
